alerts.title: US-Iran framework deal announced; Trump declares war ended, Strait of Hormuz to reopen
alerts.summary: President Trump announced a framework memorandum of understanding (MoU) with Iran declaring an immediate and complete end to all hostilities in the region, lifting of US naval blockade, and reopening of Strait of Hormuz, with full signing scheduled for Friday (Time Magazine, NBC News, Euronews, incidents feed). The announcement represents a major strategic shift following the previous day's Israeli Beirut strike and Iranian retaliation threats.

alerts.title: Trump criticizes Netanyahu's judgment; Iran deal framework jeopardized by Israeli actions
alerts.summary: President Trump explicitly stated Netanyahu has 'no fucking judgment' regarding the Beirut strike on Hezbollah positions, stating actions by Israel have placed the emerging Iran peace agreement at risk (Axios, incidents feed). Trump's public rebuke of a key US ally signals deep frustration over Israeli behavior potentially derailing negotiations.

alerts.title: Iran maintains 60% enrichment amid nuclear deal framework negotiations
alerts.summary: Iran continues uranium enrichment at 60% U-235 with estimated 1-2 week nuclear breakout window, while IAEA board passed resolution demanding Iran report uranium stocks and grant inspectors access to facilities (Reuters, Times of Israel, global_metrics). Nuclear verification crisis deepens even as political framework agreement takes shape.

alerts.title: Iran-US MoU full text circulates on OSINT channels; nuclear and proxy constraints detailed
alerts.summary: Middle East Spectator Telegram channel (~56K views) posted full details of Iran-US Phase 1 MoU declaring immediate permanent end to all hostilities in region including Lebanon upon announcement, with complete lifting of US naval blockade (telegram feed). High-engagement post with nuclear-flagged keywords confirms deal framework details now in open-source circulation.

alerts.title: Iran-linked group claims FBI drone hack; threats issued against World Cup infrastructure
alerts.summary: Iran-linked cyber group claimed responsibility for hacking FBI drone systems and threatened World Cup infrastructure in apparent retaliation for regional military actions (France 24, CBS News, global_metrics cyber headlines). Attack signals potential Iranian cyber escalation in response to perceived US-Israeli coordinated operations.

analysis_note: Strategic inflection point: Trump administration has pivoted toward Iran deal completion despite Israeli escalation, nuclear verification remains unresolved, and threat level remains critical (9/10) pending final agreement signing and proxy force de-escalation.
